pi_items.source: Python script parsing SRS.json reveals that nodes with IDs 15 and 22 exist, but no node with ID 42 (or ending in 42) exists in the document.
verifier.py uses this exact string to verify the text update of node 15.
verifier.py checks for this forbidden string, but parsing SRS.json shows '200 ms' does not appear in the original node 15 (which lacks a text attribute entirely).
verifier.py uses this exact string to verify the text update of node 22.
verifier.py checks for this forbidden string, but parsing SRS.json shows 'must' does not appear in the original node 22 (which lacks a text attribute entirely).
verifier.py checks for this string in node 42, but since node 42 doesn't exist, this check is inherently broken.
setup_task_project function in scripts/task_utils.sh and setup_task.sh confirms this is where the project is copied to in the environment.

privileged_info_summary: The task is fundamentally flawed. The metadata and instructions claim the agent must modify the text of requirements SRS-15, SRS-22, and SRS-42 based on injected review comments. However, inspecting the underlying SRS.json file reveals that node SRS-42 does not exist anywhere in the document. Furthermore, nodes SRS-15 and SRS-22 exist only as section headings (lacking any 'text' attribute), meaning the 'forbidden' original texts ('200 ms' and 'must') that the agent is supposed to replace do not exist. The setup script blindly injects the review comments into nodes 15 and 22, but fails to inject anything for 42 because it cannot be found. This makes the task unachievable as described.
